goal: Increase email campaign conversion rate to 5%

prd-001.metric_trajectory: Conversion: 1.1% (ep1 baseline) → 1.8% (ep2) → 2.1% (ep3) → 2.1% (ep4). Open rate: 18% → 21% → 22% → 22%. Click rate: 3.1% → 3.4% → 3.6% → 3.5%. Landing CVR: 8.2% → 8.0% → 8.1% → 7.9%. Plateaued on conversion — open rate and click rate gains exhausted within template-only approach; landing page untouched.

prd-002.metric_trajectory: Conversion: 2.3% (ep5) → 3.1% (ep6) → 3.8% (ep7). Open rate: 24% → 26% → 27%. Click rate: 4.2% → 4.8% → 5.1%. Landing CVR: 8.0% → 9.2% → 10.5%. Full-funnel approach working — landing page optimization unlocked new gains. Still climbing.
prd-002.peak: Conversion 3.8%, Open 27%, Click 5.1%, Landing CVR 10.5% at epoch 7

history.reason: Template-only approach plateaued — landing page is the bottleneck
Full-funnel approach addresses landing page bottleneck identified in prd-001

learnings: Subject line personalization drives open rate but has diminishing returns past 25%
Landing page CVR was the funnel bottleneck — template/email changes alone cannot push conversion past ~2.1%
Session replay data showed users bouncing at pricing section — simplified pricing in ep6 lifted landing CVR from 8% to 9.2%
A/B testing CTA placement had more impact than copy changes on click-through rate

dead_ends.approach: Emoji subject lines for open rate lift
dead_ends.reason: Open rate increased 1.2% but click rate dropped 0.8% — net negative on conversion
why_chain: WHY-1: Conversion dropped despite higher opens
WHY-3: Segment mismatch — casual browsers vs buyers
attempts_explored: Attempt 1: emoji in all segments, net negative
Attempt 2: emoji only in re-engagement segment, neutral

dead_ends.reason: Landing CVR dropped from 8.0% to 6.3% — users didn't scroll past fold
why_chain: WHY-1: CVR dropped with new page
WHY-2: Heatmap shows 72% of users never scrolled
WHY-3: Mobile users (68% of traffic) see only hero section
attempts_explored: Attempt 1: full testimonial section, CVR dropped
Attempt 2: condensed to 3 quotes above fold, CVR recovered to 7.8% but didn't beat control

dead_ends.reason: Email template changes alone cannot push conversion past ~2.1% — landing page is the funnel bottleneck
why_chain: WHY-1: Conversion plateaued at 2.1% despite open/click gains
WHY-2: Landing CVR flat at ~8% — email changes don't affect post-click experience
WHY-3: prd-001 has no stories addressing landing page — approach scope excludes the bottleneck
attempts_explored: Attempt 2: CTA redesign, click rate +0.5% but landing CVR unchanged
Attempt 3: send-time optimization, open rate +1% but no conversion lift

ceiling_evidence.best_achieved: Conversion 2.1% (target 5%) — best across 4 epochs of template-only optimization
ceiling_evidence.ceiling_constraint: prd-001 scope limited to email template; landing page CVR (8%) is the binding constraint and cannot be addressed within template-only stories
ceiling_evidence.best_instance_check: Best-performing segment (returning customers) reached 2.8% — still well below 5% target, confirming ceiling is approach-wide, not segment-specific

notes: Landing CVR climbing — story 4 targets checkout flow optimization
Session replay data from ep7 available in workspace/results/epoch-7/sessions/
Conversion at 3.8%, target is 5% — need ~1.2pp more, landing CVR and checkout are the remaining levers
